title: Phlomoides botschantzevii
taxonomy: Phlomoides botschantzevii belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification places it within the order Lamiales, which consists of flowering plants that are predominantly aromatic. At the genus level, Phlomoides botschantzevii is a species of the genus Phlomoides, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily native to Asia. The species name 'botschantzevii' is a tribute to the botanist who discovered or described it, Botschantzev. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics, growth habits, and genetic makeup.

Its roots and leaves are reported to possess antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating wounds and infections.
